strengths: Clear and appropriate scenario with an identified client and evidence of consultation through meeting references and task records
Wellâjustified product rationale linking client pain-points to solution features and IB CS skills
Comprehensive and measurable success criteria covering security, usability, performance, and compatibility
weaknesses: Client consultation lacks full primary evidence (e.g., transcripts) for thoroughness
Rationale would be stronger with explicit comparison to alternative tools or approaches
Success criteria could include quantifiable targets (e.g., page-load time thresholds)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The planning is solid: the scenario and client are clearly identified, consultation is evidenced through meeting logs, the rationale is coherent, and success criteria are comprehensive and measurable. However, the depth of primary consultation evidence is not âthoroughâ, and the rationale could be strengthened with alternative comparisons and quantifiable targets. These factors place it just below full marks.

strengths: High complexity and ingenuity: integration of JWT auth, encrypted key storage, Selenium automation, AI-generated responses, and relational database
Appropriate and effective use of professional tools and libraries (SQLAlchemy, JWT, cryptography, Selenium, OpenAI API, FastAPI)
Modular design with clear function catalogue demonstrating logical separation and code structure
weaknesses: Lack of proper citation and source attribution for third-party libraries and tutorials
Omission of some edge-case handling details (e.g., JSON error branches in API key functions)
No CI/CD or deployment pipeline documentation to illustrate operational maturity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The development exhibits significant complexity and effective use of advanced tools, meriting a strong top-band score. However, missing citations, incomplete edge-case handling, and absence of deployment workflows reduce the mark slightly from the maximum possible.

strengths: Detailed task record with purposes, outcomes, and links to planning criteria
Comprehensive design overview including schema, user-flow charts, page mock-ups, and function catalogue
Exhaustive unit test plan covering valid, boundary, and error conditions for all major functions
weaknesses: Some âTarget Completion Dateâ fields are blank, weakening timeline credibility
Inconsistent notation in the database schema (primary key symbols) and missing index definitions
Minor typos and commented markers in pseudocode reduce maintainability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The solution overview demonstrates a clear development process: the task log is well structured, the design overview is comprehensive, and the test plan is exemplary. Minor omissions in task dates, schema notation, and pseudocode prevent full marks, but overall the work is of high quality and deserves near-top marks.

strengths: Modular architecture suggests a foundation for future expansion and feature addition
weaknesses: No runtime evidence (screenshots or video) to verify actual product functionality
Extensibility is only implied; no explicit description of how new features would be integrated
Lack of demonstration undermines confidence in product operation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Without any demonstration of a working product, functionality can only be assumed partially, placing it in the 1â2 band. The modular design hints at extensibility, but the absence of concrete examples or integration plans limits the score to the lower half of the scale.

weaknesses: No evaluation of the implemented solution against success criteria
Zero evidence of client or adviser feedback (no quotes, surveys, or meeting excerpts)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: There is no evaluative content: the student did not assess the product against success criteria, include client feedback, or propose realistic future recommendations. This absence of any evaluation elements necessitates a score of zero.

strandSelections.reasoning: A database schema, relationship descriptions, user-flow chart, page mock-ups and a comprehensive function catalogue together form a clear picture of how the product will be built. Minor omissions in overall architecture diagrams keep it just below a perfect 6.

The rationale links the solution to observed client pain-points, explains how automation bridges gaps in existing processes, and explicitly relates features to skills gained in IB CS. The justification is coherent and complete, though it could be strengthened by explicit comparison with alternatives; hence the low end of the top band.

The student employs SQL Alchemy, JWT libraries, cryptography, Selenium, OpenAI APIs and FastAPI-style endpoints, showing broad and effective use of existing professional tools. Some implementation specifics (e.g., CI/CD) are not detailed, so a mid-top-band score is appropriate.

The submission provides an extensive table of 60+ unit tests covering valid, boundary and error conditions for every major function. Inputs and expected outputs are explicit, fulfilling the descriptor for a detailed and complete outline test plan.
The documentation stops at deployment; no forward-looking, well-justified recommendations for future improvements are written.

Function tables describe logic clearly, but citations or links to external code samples, tutorials, or libraries are missing. Techniques are explained but not comprehensively sourced, placing the work in the middle of the 5-8 band.

The task table lists twenty numbered tasks with purpose, outcomes, time estimates and criterion links, illustrating the development flow. Missing actual dates and some incomplete cells prevent it from being fully âdetailed and completeâ, so it sits at the top of the 3-4 band.

The solution integrates JWT authentication, encrypted API key storage, Selenium web automation, AIâgenerated responses and a relational database. This blend demonstrates significant ingenuity and multi-layer complexity, although full source listings and working evidence are not shown; hence the lower end of the top band.

Ten well-defined, measurable criteria span security, usability, performance, and compatibility. Each criterion is specific enough to drive later testing, satisfying the requirement for a comprehensive range.
Although the task list mentions âclient feedback meetingsâ, no actual feedback evidence (quotes, screenshots, or surveys) is supplied.

The modular architecture (separate tables, functions, API key management) suggests future expansion is possible, but without working code evidence extensibility cannot be called straightforward. The submission therefore aligns with âpossible but difficultâ.

The student clearly identifies a real client (older cousin) and summarises the clientâs frustrations. Meeting references (Appendix A) and a multi-stage task record confirm some consultation, although the actual transcripts are absent. The investigation is therefore present and shows evidence, but the depth of primary evidence is not "thorough".

No runtime video or screenshots demonstrating an operational system are provided. While design artefacts exist, evidence of an actually functioning product is absent, so only partial functionality can be assumed.
No section evaluating the finished product against the earlier success criteria is included.

annotations.comment: Function catalogue is extensive, showing ingenuity in modular design and clear logic separation.
The core AI function âgenerate_application_responsesâ is well documented, reflecting high complexity and effective tool integration.
Despite references to client meetings, no direct feedback excerpts or survey results are included; add verbatim client comments.
No future improvement recommendations are provided; include realistic next steps (e.g., multiâthreading, external job board integration).
Sources for thirdâparty libraries and tutorials are not cited; include references to demonstrate research and proper attribution.
The problem definition effectively presents a clear scenario with a specific client context, demonstrating solid initial planning and client consultation.
The rationale links IB skills to solution features, but could be improved by comparing with alternative tools or approaches to strengthen justification.
Success criteria list is comprehensive and measurable across security, usability, performance, and compatibility, supporting later evaluation.
Consider adding quantifiable targets (e.g., maximum page load time) to each success criterion to enable precise evaluation.
The task record is well structured with purposes and criterion links, illustrating planning flow clearly.
Several âTarget Completion Dateâ cells are blank, hindering timeline credibility; fill these to strengthen record of tasks.
The database schema diagram covers all tables but uses inconsistent notation for primary keys; standardize symbols (e.g., âPKâ).
Tables include most fields but omit explicit index definitions for performance. Add index details for heavyâuse columns like user_id and job_url.
Pseudocode lacks foreignâkey relationship notation; specify cascade rules and uniqueness in code comments to reflect schema constraints.
Flowchart figure is clear and provides a holistic view of user navigation; it enhances understanding of system flow.
Mermaid code contains commented markers and minor typos (âDecision_Loginâ); clean and verify syntax for better maintainability.
Some function logic descriptions omit edgeâcase handling (e.g., missing JSON branches in API key functions); include these details.
No runtime screenshots or demonstration URL provided, making it difficult to verify actual product functionality.
Modular design suggests extensibility, but the document should explicitly describe how new features (e.g., OAuth integration) would be plugged in.
The test plan is impressively exhaustive, with numerous edge and error cases covering all key functions.
No section evaluates the implemented solution against the established success criteria, which is essential for Criterion E.
